title: 採用者ツリーからテンプレート release-digests.json を除外する

sources.reason: Remediation cycle plan, Task 7: adopter_release_manifest_boundary acceptance criteria.
TEMPLATE_SUPPLY_CHAIN_BASELINES constant and tree_copy_pairs method define the current exclusion list.
Existing test_installed_distribution_contains_pr_and_approval_wiring asserts sbom/provenance/bandit excluded but not release-digests.json.
The manifest file to be excluded: contains template-release artifact digests that are meaningless in an adopter repository.

riskAssessment.reason: Adding release-digests.json to TEMPLATE_SUPPLY_CHAIN_BASELINES exclusion set. Minimal scope: one constant change in installer, one test assertion added. No destructive change to existing adopter trees; only affects fresh installations.

executionDecision.reason: Scope, acceptance, sources, and verification are all specified. Unknowns are empty. Implementation is a single-constant addition plus test coverage.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

intent.problem: The installer's TEMPLATE_SUPPLY_CHAIN_BASELINES exclusion set omits .ai/cockpit/release-digests.json. This manifest records digests of template release artifacts (install.sh, release.json, sbom.json, provenance.json, requirements-dev.lock). Copying it into an adopter repository creates a stale manifest that claims to prove artifacts absent from the adopter tree (e.g. it references a fixed sourceCommit and releaseTag from the template). The adopter tree thereby contains a file with semantically false integrity claims. The plan (Task 7, P1-4) requires this boundary to be enforced by the installer, by the adoption readiness check, and to be reflected in documentation.
constraints: Do not change existing excluded files: bandit_low_risk_baseline.json, provenance.json, sbom.json remain excluded.
Do not alter check_release_distribution.py or check_supply_chain.py — those operate on the template repository's own supply-chain, not on adoption.
The exclusion applies only during copy_tree; upgrade path already skips supply-chain baselines through the same set.
Do not add adoption-readiness failures for release-digests.json absence — the point is to exclude it, not require adopters to have their own.
Documentation update must describe the boundary, not prescribe adopter-owned manifest creation.
intent.rationale: Adding release-digests.json to TEMPLATE_SUPPLY_CHAIN_BASELINES is the minimal correct fix: the same mechanism that already excludes sbom.json and provenance.json (template supply-chain evidence) naturally covers the release digests manifest, which is equally template-specific. A single-constant change, matched by a test assertion and a documentation note, closes the boundary without introducing new modes or flags.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Fresh install: release-digests.json is absent from adopter tree
scenarioCoverage.reason: test_installer.py assertion to be added: assert not (tmp_path / '.ai' / 'cockpit' / 'release-digests.json').exists()

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Fresh install: other excluded files remain excluded
scenarioCoverage.reason: Existing assertions for sbom.json, provenance.json, bandit_low_risk_baseline.json must remain unchanged after edit.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Upgrade path: release-digests.json exclusion enforced via same TEMPLATE_SUPPLY_CHAIN_BASELINES set
scenarioCoverage.reason: TEMPLATE_SUPPLY_CHAIN_BASELINES is shared between copy_tree and managed_copy_pairs; adding the entry covers both install and upgrade conflict detection.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: E2E smoke: installed adopter tree has no stale manifest
scenarioCoverage.reason: test_adoption_e2e.py assertion that release-digests.json is not present in installed adopter tree.

acceptance: A fresh install does not copy .ai/cockpit/release-digests.json to the adopter tree.
The resulting adopter tree contains no manifest that claims to prove template-release artifacts absent from that tree.
test_installer.py asserts absence of release-digests.json in the installed target.
test_adoption_e2e.py includes an assertion that verifies the absence of the stale manifest.
Installation documentation and adoption.md describe that release-digests.json is a template supply-chain file not copied to adopters.
make quality passes with no coverage regression.
guidelines: Add ('cockpit', 'release-digests.json') to TEMPLATE_SUPPLY_CHAIN_BASELINES frozenset; do not introduce a new mechanism.
Assertions in test_installer.py must mirror the existing pattern for sbom.json and provenance.json.
Documentation changes must be minimal: one sentence clarifying release-digests.json is template-only, aligned with the existing description of sbom.json and provenance.json.
Do not remove or change existing exclusions.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: adoption.md and adoption.ja.md are restricted governance paths. The edits are documentation-only: one sentence added to describe the release-digests.json exclusion boundary. No behavioral change; no removal of existing guidance.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
